title: Credit Risk Analyst
description: This is Adyen Adyen provides payments, data, and financial products in a single solution for customers like Meta, Uber, H&M, and Microsoft - making us the financial technology platform of choice. At Adyen, everything we do is engineered for ambition. For our teams, we create an environment with opportunities for our people to succeed, backed by the culture and support to ensure they are enabled to truly own their careers. We are motivated individuals who tackle unique technical challenges at scale and solve them as a team. Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster. Credit Risk Analyst - Financial Products As a Credit Risk Analyst based in Chicago, you will be part of a growing team responsible for evaluating, managing, and scaling credit risk across Adyen’s expanding suite of financial products. The team plays a critical role in assessing credit risk, underwriting SME and enterprise merchants, and ensuring that portfolio performance remains within Adyen’s risk appetite, while enabling sustainable growth for our merchants. Beyond day-to-day underwriting and portfolio management, the team owns the development and operation of credit risk frameworks for new and evolving financial products (e.g. Adyen Capital). Working closely with Commercial, Product, Compliance, and other cross-functional partners, you will help design scalable, data-driven risk processes that support Adyen’s global growth ambitions. The Credit Risk team is in an early stage of its journey, offering significant opportunities to shape processes, influence product direction, and grow alongside the business. You will join an international, diverse team that values ownership, pragmatism, and collaboration, and operates across regions and cultures. The ideal candidate combines strong problem-solving skills, clear and direct communication, and deep financial expertise to deliver thoughtful risk assessments and contribute to strategic recommendations on credit policies, frameworks, and practices. Please note that this role is a hybrid, 3x a week position in Chicago. Base salary compensation for this role in Chicago is- $85,000.00-$110,000.00. The total compensation package also includes equity (RSUs). What You’ll Do Perform the credit underwriting and portfolio monitoring of different financial products, including loans to SMBs and other credit products to enterprise customers; Analyze the impact of business model, industry and macro-economic developments on the credit portfolios and the credit risk offering of Adyen; Develop and implement credit risk strategies to enhance portfolio performance while ensuring alignment with risk appetite and business objectives; Drive continuous improvement of our global and US-specific credit risk underwriting and monitoring framework, including policies, procedures and practices; Provide credit risk expertise to a variety of stakeholders to guarantee that credit risk is properly considered in new commercial product developments; Lead projects from beginning to end, and find optimal solutions that benefit our customers and improve our risk management practices; Who You Are Financial Expertise- Minimum of 3+ years of experience in commercial credit underwriting, preferably within banking, fintech, or SMEs credit lending-driven environments. Strong analytical capabilities with demonstrated ability to interpret financial statements, assess borrower quality, and identify key risk drivers, trends, and early-warning indicators. Proven experience managing and assessing credit portfolios across products such as SMB lending, working capital solutions, overdrafts, charge cards, and/or asset-backed financing. Experience operating in high-volume, scalable credit environments is a strong plus, including exposure to payments, automation, and data-driven decisioning frameworks. Ability to balance risk and return, applying sound judgment to make credit decisions in line with defined risk appetite and business objectives. Familiarity with U.S. regulatory frameworks and guidelines related to credit risk; hands-on regulatory experience is a strong plus but not required. Mindset- Results-driven and outcomes-focused, with a strong sense of ownership and accountability for decisions and impact. Comfortable operating in a fast-scaling environment, contributing to the design of robust processes and building with automation and scalability in mind. Adaptable and resilient, able to adjust quickly to changing priorities, product offerings, and risk profiles. Collaborative team player with a low-ego, high-accountability mindset who values constructive challenge and shared success. Communication Clear and effective communicator who is able to communicate straight and to the point. Comfortable engaging with a wide range of stakeholders, both internally and externally, across functions and seniority levels. Capable of navigating complex and sensitive conversations with regulators, merchants, and partners, translating risk considerations into clear, actionable messages. Our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion commitments Our unique approach is a product of our diverse perspectives. This diversity of backgrounds and cultures is essential in helping us maintain our momentum. Our business and technical challenges are unique, and we need as many different voices as possible to join us in solving them - voices like yours. No matter who you are or where you’re from, we welcome you to be your true self at Adyen. Studies show that women and members of underrepresented communities apply for jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. Does this sound like you? If so, Adyen encourages you to reconsider and apply. We look forward to your application! What’s next?** Ensuring a smooth and enjoyable candidate experience is critical for us. We aim to get back to you regarding your application within 5 business days. Our interview process tends to take about 4 weeks to complete, but may fluctuate depending on the role. Learn more about our hiring process here. Don’t be afraid to let us know if you need more flexibility. Adyen is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, national origin, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, marital status or any legally protected status. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

You will play a pivotal role in supporting a group of ~300 employees within DPR’s Technology & Innovation (T&I) team responsible for leading DPR’s IT, Enterprise & Construction Technology and Data teams. The T&I Finance Team manages the overall budget and forecasts using our financial solutions and are responsible for the IT Burden invoicing, expense reconciliation, invoicing, and other key items. Key Responsibilities T&I Finance is responsible for managing a multi-million-dollar budget and producing financial analytics to DPR CFO. This individual will also be responsible for T&I accounting duties such as managing new vendors, invoicing, IT Burden Billing and finance analysis including Financial & Tax Reports, assisting in initiatives where our T&I teams are included and assisting in reporting to T&I and Corporate Leadership teams. JUNIOR FINANCIAL ANALYST DUTIES Assist Financial Analyst and Controller in any budget or financial analysis that may be needed. Assist the financial analyst with continuous improvement in financial processes and reporting Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop and implement new requirements, including supporting T&I and overall DPR Initiatives Creating and producing IT Burden invoices, monthly tax and financial reports. Support monthly forecast meetings Software & Consulting Contract Management Resource Management operations PROJECT ACCOUNTANT DUTIES Responsible for ensuring all invoices for T&I are properly allocated including amortization. New vendor management for T&I Liaison for Shared Services (AP/AR & Payroll) Payroll – T&I New Hire onboarding including time cards and labor transfers Shared Services T&I Vendor Invoice management Expense management auditing Liese with IT Field Engineers for internet connections at regional offices & jobsites Required Qualifications We are looking for a flexible, detail-oriented team player with the ability to manage multiple tasks, produce quality work, and consistently meet deadlines. The successful candidate will possess: Ability to resolve conflicts and influence partners Ability to create and support team morale Excellent verbal and written communication skills Ownership – totally committed to the success of the project, team, and DPR Teamwork in virtual context Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills Understanding of accounting processes Leadership, initiative, and self-motivation Project management – company-wide programs Can strategize and pivot when needed to get the job done Experience in analytics and technical reporting using Office 365 Suite of applications. Expert in financial accounting systems (DPR currently uses CMIC) Opportunities for Growth Potential for advancement to senior accounting roles, such as Senior Accountant, Financial Analyst or Controller. Opportunities for cross-training in finance, auditing, or financial planning and analysis. Support for continued education and professional development, including certifications and workshops. Involvement in strategic projects and company-wide initiatives. DPR has been nationally recognized for its strong company culture, based on a well-defined purpose “We Exist to Build Great Things,” and four core values: integrity, enjoyment, uniqueness and ever forward. A flat, titleless organization that empowers people at all levels to make decisions, DPR ranked on FORTUNE’s “100 Best Companies to Work For” list for five consecutive years. For more information, visit www.dpr.com. Anticipated starting pay range: $65,000.00- $100,000.00Nothing is more important than your health and wellness. DPR offers a variety of medical, dental, vision and wellness benefits to keep you healthy. Read more about eligibility and available benefits programsfor* skilled craft and labor or experienced professionals and recent graduates. title: Social Media Content Creator
description: The Digital Marketing Manager is a key role responsible for the strategy, execution, and optimization of the organization’s digital marketing efforts. Manage and grow social media presence by creating engaging content and strategies to increase reach, engagement, and visibility. Monitor, analyze, and report on key performance indicators (KPIs) including traffic, lead generation, CAC, ROAS, CTR, and CVR. Lead conversion rate optimization (CRO) initiatives through A/B testing and other experimentation methods to improve user experience and campaign performance. Demonstrated expertise in web analytics (GA4) and data visualization tools (Looker/Data Studio). Experience with CRM and marketing automation platforms; familiarity with Zoho is preferred. Familiarity with e-commerce platforms; experience with WooCommerce is a plus. Strong understanding of A/B testing, CRO, and lifecycle email marketing. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Ability to self-manage, prioritize tasks, and meet deadlines in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment. Basic familiarity with HTML, CSS, and content management systems (CMS). Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights.

title: Financial Planning & Analyst I-III
description: FP&A is a pivotal function that reports aggregated corporate results on a monthly/quarterly/annual basis to senior management, which is used across the organization to measure financial performance, decision-making, and course correcting activities. The Financial Planning and Analysis Analyst will research, analyze, organize, compile and report on financial and operational data to provide insights to senior management. This position will have a strong understanding of the data, trends and operational benchmarks that measure financial performance. A successful analyst will prepare reports, develop insights, and craft communications and presentations for Sr. Management, Board of Directors and other Murphy USA departments. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Assist with developing communications around monthly business reviews, annual planning and forecasting processes using data-driven decision making. Help develop scalable financial reporting processes by working with functional leaders and other teams. Research budget variances, communicating with multiple departments in the organization – collaboration skills are a must. Ad-hoc financial reporting as requested by senior management. Assist with reporting around the Annual Financial Plan and 3-5-year business view. Assist with monitoring of department level G&A budgets along with monthly reporting and quarterly forecasting responsibilities. Preparation and monitoring of Capital spending along with monthly variance reporting. Preparation and continual improvement of summary financial information relevant to BOD level reporting. Assist with monthly update and Outlook process, providing a rolling monthly view of the fiscal year's key financial and performance metrics. Develop and maintain working capital and cash flow models, consistent with actual and forecast. Calculate and monitor KPI performance relative to incentive plans. Perform any other related duties as required or assigned. QUALIFICATIONS This role requires a strong understanding of financial statements, ability to parse, organize, and develop insights from large datasets, excellent interpersonal and communication skills, keen attention to detail, ability to work in fast-paced deadline-oriented environment. Strong analytical skills are required, and experience is preferred with respect to organizing and summarizing financial information in written and tabular form. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE Broad knowledge of such fields as accounting, marketing, business administration, finance, etc. Equivalent to a four-year college degree, plus 0 to 6 months related experience and/or training, or equivalent combination of education and experience. COMMUNICATION SKILLS Written, oral and interpersonal communications skills are required, with an attention to detail to create effective presentations for internal audiences. The ability to effectively communicate information is paramount in response to questions in person-to-person and small group situations with customers, clients, public and other employees of the organization. It will be necessary to read, analyze, and understand general business/company related articles and professional journals coupled with the ability to speak effectively before groups of customers or employees. Written reports and general business correspondence are also important to this role. MATHEMATICAL SKILLS Ability to calculate figures and amounts such as discounts, interest, CAGR, WACC, percentages, and other often used financial ratios or concepts. CRITICAL THINKING SKILLS Ability to solve practical problems and deal with a variety of known variables in situations where only limited standardization exists. Ability to interpret a variety of instructions furnished in written, oral, or diagram formats. REQUIRED CERTIFICATES, LICEN Auto req ID 211927BR Store Number/Dept Number 299900015000 - FP&A (Financial Planning & Analysis) Store Zip 71730

title: AR Specialist

As members of the Optum family of businesses, we are dedicated to helping people feel their best, including our team members who create meaningful connections with patients, their families, each other and the communities we serve. As the Revenue Cycle Analyst you will perform all revenue cycle reporting and analysis for revenue cycle leadership, operational teams, and accounting. This analysis consists of daily, weekly, monthly, ad ad-hoc reports using real-time data and information (financial, statistical and other data). The results of the analysis are then used to provide revenue cycle leadership and operations management (DVPs and other operations management) with real-time feedback. You'll be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role as well as provide development for other roles you may be interested in. Required Qualifications: Bachelor's Degree 2+ years in a healthcare-related field 2+ years in relevant Professional Accounting/Financial Analysis experience Demonstrate superior analytical skills, both financial and statistical Demonstrated a natural sense of urgency in all actions Demonstrated ability to use modern accounting and financial software platforms and databases Demonstrated strong proficiency in Microsoft Office applications Preferred Qualifications: Demonstrated solid oral and written communication skills Demonstrated excellent interpersonal skills Demonstrated ability to work alongside other management personnel to achieve high levels of operating performance Demonstrated ability to influence other personnel to produce improved operating outcomes Demonstrated to be a self-starter and self-motivated, able to consistently demonstrate these qualities in a fast-paced environment Pay is based on several factors including but not limited to local labor markets, education, work experience, certifications, etc. In addition to your salary, we offer benefits such as, a comprehensive benefits package, incentive and recognition programs, equity stock purchase and 401k contribution (all benefits are subject to eligibility requirements). title: Senior Scientist, Health Economics & Outcomes Research (HEOR)
description: Job Description: Senior Scientist, Health Economics & Outcomes Research (HEOR) Work Arrangement: Hybrid - onsite 3 days per week Position Summary: The Senior Manager / Programmer, Real-World Evidence (RWE) will support the execution of real-world evidence and epidemiology studies to demonstrate unmet medical need, product value, and differentiation. This role partners closely with RWE leadership and cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality analyses, visualizations, and scientific communications that support clinical development, regulatory, safety, and market access objectives. Key Responsibilities: Execute real-world evidence (RWE) studies using large healthcare datasets in alignment with study protocols and research objectives. Extract, clean, and analyze real-world data (RWD) using SAS or R, with database programming in SQL or Python. Collaborate with RWE researchers to develop code lists, data specifications, and analytical datasets across observational databases. Perform quality assurance, code validation, and peer review of programming outputs. Draft analysis reports and support scientific publications and conference presentations. Develop data visualizations and automation tools (e.g., R Shiny, Power BI, Excel) to communicate insights to internal stakeholders. Support management of external vendors and partners for RWE projects. Engage cross-functionally with development, commercial, market access, safety, legal, and medical affairs teams. Qualifications: Graduate degree in Epidemiology, Biostatistics, Public Health, Informatics, or a related field. 3+ years of post-graduate experience in statistical programming (SAS or R) and database programming (SQL and/or Python). Experience with real-world/observational research study design, execution, and communication. Strong understanding of epidemiologic and statistical concepts (e.g., confounding, bias, incidence rates, survival analysis, regression models, propensity score methods). Experience working with US secondary data sources; international data experience is a plus. Working knowledge of oncology (solid tumors and/or hematologic malignancies). Proficiency with data visualization and dashboard tools (R Shiny, Power BI, Excel, or similar). Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to present complex findings clearly. Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, matrixed environment and collaborate across teams.

If so, we invite you to join our WHS DataTech team as an Applied Scientist and take your career to the next level! Our work spans real-time risk assessment, predictive analytics, and AI-powered insights, all aimed at creating a safer work environment at scale. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in machine learning and related fields. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand business needs and identify areas for application of machine learning. title: Senior Financial Analyst, Content Strategy & Investments
description: About Wonder Project Wonder Project is a transformative entertainment brand producing and curating premium movies and TV series for the global faith and values audience. We understand the profound impact that entertainment has on our lives and on culture itself. Our purpose is to tell stories that restore faith in things worth believing in—stories that bring audiences together and endure for generations. Guided by the principles of courage, collaboration, excellence and service, we strive to see the world through different perspectives, recognizing that empathy fosters understanding and unity. We're looking for motivated team members who are passionate about their craft and hungry to make a meaningful impact through their work. We're building something new in entertainment, and that requires creativity, curiosity, kindness and a bias towards action. If you thrive in a fast-moving environment where every contribution matters and you're energized by the opportunity to shape culture through storytelling, you'll fit right in. We welcome you to join us on this journey as we build an entertainment brand committed to restoring faith in things worth believing in. We are seeking a strategic Senior Analyst with finance and analytics expertise to join our Strategy team. This role combines financial modeling, content investment analysis, and performance analytics to guide greenlight decisions and drive profitable growth for our streaming platform. You will build valuation models for content investments, evaluate title performance across distribution windows, and provide financial recommendations that shape our content slate and platform strategy as we launch and scale Wonder. This is a unique opportunity to build the finance and strategy function at a mission-driven streaming platform from the ground up. Your greenlight models and investment analyses will directly influence what content we produce, how we value theatrical and streaming performance, and how we scale Wonder profitably. You'll work closely with creative, production, finance, and executive teams, seeing the immediate impact of your financial recommendations on real content decisions. Join a company where financial discipline meets purpose and help us prove that values-based content can captivate a global audience while building a sustainable, high-growth streaming business. Key Responsibilities Greenlight Valuation & Deal Analysis: Partner with finance and production teams to build greenlight models for all potential original content. Assess the ROI of content investments by developing financial models that incorporate data-driven inputs on title performance across distribution windows (Streaming, Theatrical Box Office, and Home Video), subscriber impact, and production costs to inform content slate decisions. Streaming Metrics and Unit Economics: Strategically evaluate key streaming platform metrics including Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC), subscriber churn rates, retention cohorts, payback periods, and customer lifetime value (LTV) to optimize marketing spend and inform content investment decisions with clear ROI frameworks. Financial Planning & Budget Support: Partner with finance leadership to support annual content budget planning, investment allocation decisions across the content slate, and ongoing forecast updates based on subscriber acquisition trends, content performance, and competitive dynamics. Content Investment Analysis: Evaluate the financial performance and ROI of original productions and licensed content by analyzing viewership metrics, cost per hour viewed, subscriber attribution, completion rates, engagement patterns, and audience retention to guide future investment priorities and assess P&L impact. Competitive Benchmarking: Monitor industry trends and competitor streaming platforms, analyzing their content strategies, investment patterns, release tactics, and financial performance to identify best practices, market opportunities, and inform our competitive positioning. Audience Insights and Segmentation: Conduct deep-dive analyses on viewer demographics, preferences, viewing hours, and behavior to identify high-value audience segments and inform content acquisition, marketing, and programming strategies that maximize engagement, drive subscriber growth, and minimize churn. A/B Testing and Experimentation: Design and analyze experiments related to content presentation, user interface, promotional strategies, and recommendation algorithms to optimize user experience, viewing hours, and retention metrics with measurable business impact. Planning & Methodology Development: Design and deploy methodologies that inform content greenlighting, audience forecasting, performance benchmarking, and investment prioritization to ensure data-driven, financially sound decision-making. Reporting and Visualization: Build comprehensive dashboards and reports for leadership, production teams, and content executives, translating complex financial and performance data into clear, actionable insights that drive decision-making around content ROI, CAC optimization, and churn reduction. Own intercompany data reporting and serve as point of contact for all data-related tasks, including oversight of all external reporting obligations to studio partners. Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field; candidates with Statistics, Data Science, or Mathematics backgrounds who have strong financial modeling capabilities are also encouraged to apply. 4-6 years of experience in finance, FP&A, strategy consulting, or business analytics with strong financial modeling capabilities, preferably within streaming media, entertainment, subscription businesses, or technology companies with exposure to content valuation, greenlight processes, CAC/LTV economics, and investment analysis. Advanced proficiency in financial modeling and Excel; strong SQL and data visualization skills (Tableau, Looker, or similar); experience with Python or R for statistical analysis is a plus. Strong understanding of streaming platform metrics, subscription business economics, content valuation frameworks, distribution window analysis, and cohort-based retention analysis, including experience building business cases and greenlight models. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to build financial models, extract insights from large complex datasets, and translate them into investment recommendations and strategic guidance. Clear communication skills with experience presenting financial analysis and data-driven recommendations to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, including creative executives and senior leadership. Passion for entertainment content and understanding of what drives viewer engagement, retention, satisfaction, and the financial dynamics of content production and distribution. Salary & Benefits: Salary: $120K - $180K Benefits, FSA, 401k The Wonder Project, Inc. is an equal employment opportunity employer. All employees and applicants are evaluated on the basis of their qualifications, consistent with applicable state and federal laws. In addition, The Wonder Project, Inc. will provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. The Wonder Project, Inc. will consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the requirements of applicable state and federal law.

title: Financial Data Analyst
description: Dice is the leading career destination for tech experts at every stage of their careers. Our client, MARS IT, is seeking the following. Apply via Dice today! MARS Solutions Group is looking for an experienced Data Analyst located in Boston MA. Our client is a leading independent asset management firm looking for high-quality talent to make a difference. They are known to respect a traditional work week and often extend contracts for added job security and stability. We are seeking a highly detail-oriented and experienced Data Analyst for a critical data transformation project. This role focuses on the migration of Benchmark (Index) and Security/Issuer Master data from a legacy system to a new data platform. Unlike a pure engineering role, this position requires a high degree of business knowledge. The ideal candidate understands the complexities of financial reference data and can bridge the gap between technical migration and business utility. You will be responsible for ensuring the integrity of our data through rigorous cleanup, reconciliation, and UAT. Responsibilities Data Analysis & Cleanup: Analyze large datasets of security and benchmark data (Equities, Fixed Income, Derivatives, Indices) to identify gaps, duplicates, and inconsistencies prior to migration. Perform root-cause analysis on bad data and execute manual or bulk cleanup procedures. Map data attributes between the legacy source system and the target platform, ensuring accurate translation of business logic. Testing & Validation: Lead the reconciliation process between the old and new platforms to ensure data parity. Design and execute detailed test cases for security setup, pricing feeds, and benchmark composition. Identify, log, and track data defects; work closely with the technical implementation team to resolve migration errors. Business Support & Documentation: Validate that the new platform meets the consumption needs of downstream systems and consumers. Develop and document new processes and operational workflows. Document data dictionaries, exception handling procedures, and operational controls. Act as a subject matter expert (SME) for business users during the transition period. Candidate We are seeking a highly motivated Operations Analyst who can work independently on complex processes. The ideal candidate will have knowledge of financial products and valuation coupled with a strong background in data management, including experience with SQL, PowerApps, and Python. Required Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, or a related field 5+ years of experience in Data Operations, Data Governance, or Investment Operations within an Asset Manager or Hedge Fund. Deep understanding of financial instruments (Equities, Bonds, Futures, Options) and Reference Data (Issuers, Corporate Actions, Ratings, Classifications). Familiarity with major index providers (e.g., MSCI, S&P, Bloomberg, FTSE) and how benchmark data is structured (constituents, weights, returns). Advanced Excel proficiency (VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables) is required. Proficiency in SQL for data querying and analysis is highly preferred. Preferred Qualifications Familiarity with accounting and valuation of various asset classes including structured products and derivatives Experience with Accounting and Trading Systems; APX, CRD, Bloomberg Experience with major EDM (Enterprise Data Management) platforms (e.g., GoldenSource, IHS Markit/S&P EDM, SimCorp). About MARS Solutions Group:** MARS Solutions Group provides a range of opportunities for meaningful work by understanding that employment fit is a combination of people, process, and technology. We leverage our experienced and compassionate team to bring humanity to matching you with the right advanced technology role, and stay connected with you to help you attain your professional goals.

title: Data Engineer
description: Are you a Southwest Employee? Current Southwest Employees should apply for jobs using the internal My Career & Referrals portal. Every role has an impact on our patients’ lives and you have the opportunity to make a difference. We are looking for a dedicated Lead Data Scientist like you to be a part of our team. Job Summary and Qualifications Job Summary At HCA Healthcare, we are committed to providing our patients and care providers with the highest quality of healthcare services. HCA’s Care Transformation and Innovation (CT&I) team delivers a step change in that direction through clinically led integration of digital and AI technologies into care. Data Scientists within CT&I will play a critical role in helping us achieve this goal by actively solving for and implementing a broad range of data science solutions to drive transformational change. This includes delivery of data science products from incubation to deployment to monitoring; including solutioning for the business need, data analysis, feature engineering, building and validating models, deploying solutions to enterprise production platforms, and monitoring model performance and reliability. This individual will be responsible for actively delivering production grade data science products; including implementing best practices, frameworks, tooling, and documentation. They will be expected to bring hands-on expertise in predictive analytics, classification, image recognition, NLP, anomaly detection, machine learning, EDA, feature engineering, optimization, statistics, and generalized business problem solving by creatively applying AI/ML. What you will do: DATA INJECTION: Design and implement incoming data for feature engineering and machine learning FEATURE CREATION AND TRANSFORMATION: Create features with usable predictive power by using domain knowledge of healthcare data, statistics, and data science FEATURE EXTRACTION AND SELECTION: Extract features via cluster analysis, text analytics, principal components analysis and related methodologies to identify useful information without distorting original relationships or significant information in order to performance tune and promote scalability of the model EXPLORATORY DATA ANALYSIS: Conduct data exploration and analysis to identify relevant patterns, trends, and relationships; understand the data deeply to appropriately align the data features with the appropriate machine learning methodologies MODEL EXPERIMENTATION: Apply various statistical and machine learning techniques to develop, tune, and optimize predictive modeling to align with the use case requirements DELIVERY: Deploy code using best practices DOCUMENTATION: Document and present data science strategies and insights and solutions MONITORING: Build and maintain pipelines, code, and processes to monitor the proper functioning of enterprise grade data science products DATA AND OUTPUT QUALITY: Ensure high quality and integrity throughout the entire data science process PRODUCT DEVELOPMENT MINDSET: Collaborate with stakeholders, data scientists, data engineers, and product managers to build and deliver high-quality data products What qualifications you will need: 6+ years of overall experience in various aspects of data science and machine learning (for Lead level) Expert experience in SQL and Python Experience with structured and unstructured data (ie. tabular, text, images, video, etc) Experience with SQL relational and non-relational databases Experience with data processing and ETL tools (e.g Apache Spark) Experience with data visualization and data monitoring tools Experience with modern software engineering practices (e.g. automated testing, continuous integration and continuous development) Experience with Docker Experience with cloud deployments in GCP or another cloud platform Experience with Agile development methods and tools (e.g. Azure Dev Ops) Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across multiple teams and stakeholders Ability to tell a story using data and insights that drives action and change Domain knowledge of healthcare data preferred Expertise in healthcare protocols and formats such as HL7, FHIR, DICOM preferred Expert in the regulatory aspects of the healthcare domain preferred Experience in GCP platform preferred "Good people beget good people."- Dr. Thomas Frist, Sr. HCA Healthcare Co-Founder We are a family 270,000 dedicated professionals! Submit your resume today to join our community of caring! We are an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

title: Financial Analyst
description: At Clark Pacific, the Financial Analyst will support the Financial Planning & Analysis team and Manufacturing groups by providing financial analysis, reporting, forecasting, and operational support. This role partners closely with Finance, Manufacturing Leadership, Engineering, and Operations to support budgeting, forecasting, job cost analysis, KPI reporting, and continuous improvement initiatives across the organization. Competitive Compensation Range: $90,000 annually, depending on experience. Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable qualified individuals to perform the essential functions. Exercise a high level of professionalism and confidentiality when working with staff, projects, and proprietary information. Participate in the development of complex financial models for financial statement forecasts, cash flow, job cost projections, and other analyses. Own and continuously develop Excel-based manufacturing KPI dashboards showcasing weekly performance across labor, output, safety, and product-specific metrics. Support the company’s annual forecasting and budget process, including monthly reporting, variance analysis, consolidations, and ad hoc research. Assist with the monthly financial close process through reporting and the development of tools and models to drive process efficiency. Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports with commentary, insights, and variance analysis for leadership. Analyze and report on weekly labor costs and develop tools to support future headcount planning, accurate time reporting, and labor KPIs. Function as a financial partner for engineering and operating teams to better understand costs to date and project future performance. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop standard process documentation and identify opportunities for improvement and efficiency. Support leadership in preparing presentations for monthly financial reviews, strategic initiatives, and special projects. Utilize Microsoft Excel, Power BI, Power Automate, SQL, and ERP systems to report on performance and develop workflows for operational teams. Maintain professionalism and confidentiality when working with teammates, projects, and proprietary and confidential financial information. Other duties as assigned. Experience and Education: Bachelor’s degree in Finance or Accounting. Three years of experience as a Financial Analyst or similar role with experience in financial modeling, budgeting, and forecasting. Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel. Experience in a manufacturing or construction environment preferred. Knowledge of job cost accounting, Power BI, SQL, or Vista by Viewpoint is a plus. Strong analytical, organizational, and communication skills. Core Values: Determination: Meet challenges head on. Innovation: Always look for better. Well-Being: Seek the success, health and happiness of all. Benefits: Competitive compensation, being paid on a weekly basis. Health, Dental and Vision Insurance with the options of using Kaiser and VSP for vision. Medical FSA. Dependent Care FSA. Employer paid life insurance paid by Clark Pacific. Voluntary Ancillary Benefits such as Critical Illness, Accident Insurance and Whole Life insurance. 401k matching 100% of the first 3%, then 50% of the next 2%. Benefits will be subject to a waiting period.* Interested? Want to learn more? If you’re a hard-working nice person who is smart and curious, and think that this position is right for you, we want to hear from you. Please apply and let’s begin the journey. Welcome to Clark Pacific. Let’s do great things together.

The responsibilities of the individual in this position include: Support of Consulting Directors in creation of quarterly materials, product presentations, market commentaries, competitor insights, data searches, etc. Create/review/analyze the underlying data around quarterly competitor analysis for distribution to Sales & Service teams Create/review commentary for market and investment products Design and create customized analyses and responses to ad-hoc inquiries, to include index returns, data queries, competitor analysis, market trends, fund flows, etc. Work in close partnership with Sales and Service Teams to provide client service related to Russell Investments products and competitor analyses Other duties as assigned The successful candidate will have extensive demonstrable skills and experiences, including the following: Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related field CFA or progress towards CFA preferred FINRA licensed or willing to obtain the SIE, Series 7, and Series 66 within 180 days. 2-5 years of experience working directly with clients/ investors in either a consulting, sales, service , or analytical capacity Strong knowledge of global capital markets, the wealth management industry, investment vehicles commonly used by financial advisors (e.g., mutual funds, model strategies, separately managed accounts), and investment process review Proven technical and system skills (e.g., solid knowledge of financial concepts and performance measurement standards and the mathematics behind them; knowledge of Morningstar Direct, YCharts a plus; strong proficiency with Excel, PowerPoint, Word, and related tools) Proven analytical thinking, including exploring all sources of information and providing honest analysis for effective, timely decisions Strong communication skills with team members, colleagues, and clients, and the ability to find engaging ways to explain complex concepts in a simple and easy-to-understand manner Ability to work under pressure and juggle multiple projects simultaneously in a team environment Hands-on experience working with a Consulting Director with the opportunity to potentially progress to consulting director. title: IT PMO Financial Analyst
description: Our client, a globally recognized leader in the automotive industry, is undergoing a large-scale digital transformation backed by strong executive investment in technology. As IT initiatives expand globally, the PMO is seeking a detail-oriented IT PMO Financial Analyst to support the financial operations of a complex, enterprise-wide project portfolio. This role is highly focused on financial governance, invoice and PO management, reporting, and stakeholder collaboration. You will serve as a key financial partner to IT leadership, ensuring accurate tracking of spend, maintaining compliance with financial processes, and delivering clear, data-driven insights across the IT portfolio. Key Responsibilities Own and manage invoice processing and PO approval workflows, ensuring timely approvals, accurate coding, and alignment to project budgets. Partner with IT leaders, finance teams, and cross-functional stakeholders to track and reconcile project spend against approved budgets. Maintain detailed records to support audits, accrual tracking, and financial compliance requirements. Support capital planning, budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis for global IT initiatives. Prepare and distribute recurring and ad hoc financial reports, dashboards, and portfolio summaries highlighting spend, forecasts, KPIs, and budget risks. Track project accruals, open commitments, and resource-related expenses to ensure accurate month-end and quarter-end reporting. Collaborate with stakeholders to improve financial processes, streamline reporting, and enhance visibility into IT investment performance. Leverage advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, financial modeling, reconciliations) to analyze data and present actionable insights. title: Healthcare Data Scientist – Claims, Quality, & Provider Performance
description: Vālenz® Health is the platform to simplify healthcare – the destination for employers, payers, providers and members to reduce costs, improve quality, and elevate the healthcare experience. The Valenz mindset and culture of innovation combine to create a distinctly different approach to an inefficient, uninspired health system. With fully integrated solutions, Valenz engages early and often to execute across the entire patient journey – from care navigation and management to payment integrity, plan performance and provider verification. With a 99% client retention rate, we elevate expectations to a new level of efficiency, effectiveness and transparency where smarter, better, faster healthcare is possible. About This Opportunity: As a Healthcare Data Scientist at Valenz, you’ll work hands-on with large, complex healthcare claims and provider datasets to ensure data accuracy and integrity. You’ll continuously dig into the data, investigate anomalies, and contribute to models that inform how we evaluate provider performance, care quality, and risk. Using SQL and Python, you’ll identify and resolve data issues, apply statistical methods, and deliver clear, actionable insights to support smarter decision-making across the organization. This is a highly analytical and collaborative role with real impact on how care is measured, delivered, and improved. Things You’ll Do Here: Utilize reference data sources and advanced validation techniques to ensure accuracy, completeness, and integrity of healthcare claims data Assess provider scoring methodologies, identify discrepancies, and develop insights to optimize coverage accuracy Design, develop, and maintain retrospective models for historical performance analysis, and predictive models to identify high-risk patients and prioritize actionable interventions Translate complex analytical findings into clear, actionable insights, and effectively communicate results and recommendations to internal stakeholders and external clients Aggregate and reconcile multiple data sources, applying statistical and computational methods to verify data integrity, identify anomalies, and implement necessary corrections Execute comprehensive data analysis workflows, evaluate outcomes, and implement enhancements to improve model accuracy and data reliability Review and optimize claim-matching processes, troubleshoot inconsistencies, and resolve data discrepancies to maintain high-quality outputs Investigate data quality issues, conduct root cause analysis, document findings, and deliver actionable recommendations for process improvements Collaborate with cross-functional teams to enhance analytical tools, refine methodologies, and implement scalable solutions that improve efficiency and analytical capabilities Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform essential duties. What You’ll Bring to the Team: Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, Computer Science, Finance, or Economics) 7+ or more years of experience analyzing healthcare claims data, including complex and imperfect datasets Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking skills with proven ability to uncover root causes Proficiency in SQL for querying and manipulating data; familiarity with Python or similar data tools preferred Advanced Excel skills, including PivotTables and complex formulas, for data modeling and reporting Knowledge of healthcare network rosters, provider data, and quality measurement methodologies Exceptional attention to detail with a commitment to accuracy, data validation, and quality assurance Highly organized and self-motivated, with the ability to manage multiple priorities independently while collaborating effectively with others A plus if you have… Master’s degree Where You’ll Work: This is a fully remote position, and we’ll provide all the necessary equipment! Work Environment: You’ll need a quiet workspace that is free from distractions Technology: Reliable internet connection—if you can use streaming services, you’re good to go! Security: Adherence to company security protocols, including the use of VPNs, secure passwords, and company-approved devices/software Location: You must be US based, in a location where you can work effectively and comply with company policies such as HIPAA Why You'll Love Working Here Valenz is proud to be recognized by Inc. 5000 as one of America’s fastest-growing private companies. Our team is committed to delivering on our promise to engage early and often for smarter, better, faster healthcare . With this commitment, you’ll find an engaged culture – one that stands strong, vigorous, and healthy in all we do. title: Sr. Manager, SEO
description: Reporting to: Director, Ecommerce Job Description: Lumens is hiring a Sr. Manager, SEO to drive growth in our SEO program and lead a small team of passionate marketers. Optimizing organic traffic and revenue has always been core to our marketing strategy, and all levels of the company are vested in the success of SEO. The Sr. Manager, SEO is a key leader on the eCommerce team and will shape our future SEO/AIO strategy across technical SEO, content, and backlinking initiatives. This role will develop a program that drives growth in organic revenue and ensures Lumens is recognized as a leader and authority in our space. This data-driven and analytical SEO leader will establish targets for key SEO & AIO metrics, and measure performance improvements against those targets. Responsibilities: Meet or exceed revenue and growth goals Define and align SEO/AIO strategy across Marketing, Merchandising, Product, Technology, and Site Experience teams Lead the Technical SEO/AIO roadmap planning process, converting strategies into a single product delivery roadmap that is aligned with program goals. Develop and communicate detailed Technical SEO business requirements Research SEO/AIO best practices and Google algorithm updates to understand the implications of site projects; partner with the Product and Technology teams to communicate SEO best practices for web development; participate in the site release process to QA projects in the releases for SEO compatibility Develop onsite product and content optimization plans with the Brand and Site teams Lead, develop and measure the SEO/AIO content strategy Lead, manage and develop a small team Create a focused backlinking strategy and execution plan to obtain high quality backlinks from high domain authority sites. Own SEO/AIO related tools, and constantly leverage to monitor SEO-related issues, performance, and program opportunities Establish targets for key SEO/AIO metrics, measure performance improvements against those targets; develop monthly reporting to show progress against goals Responsible for team career development and morale Inform company of industry updates; be proactive in SEO/AIO research and engage with SEO blogs / forums to keep ahead of the curve and up to date on industry changes. Core Characteristics: Ability to develop SEO/AIO strategy, break down to achievable tasks/projects, set goals, regularly measure progress against goals, and reprioritize as needed Results oriented with a growth mindset Strong organizational skills Capable of working across multiple software applications and projects (eg., SEMrush, Botify, GSC, AirOps) Takes initiative; is resourceful in finding solutions Impactful and able to influence and drive tech prioritization Excellent verbal and written communication skills Comfortable in a dynamic, growing company environment A personal interest in modern design, furniture and home furnishings is encouraged Qualifications: 8+ years of SEO experience, within e-commerce / retail space Demonstrated strength in analytical abilities Team management experience Experience in working cross-functionally effectively in a mid-size organization Location: Willingness to work out of the corporate office located in San Francisco / Sacramento (with some work from home flexibility). Compensation: $120,000 to $140,000 annually + bonus Benefits: access to health benefits + 401k + 3 weeks annual paid time off About Lumens: As North America’s premier destination for design aficionados, architects, interior designers, and contract professionals, Lumens offers a discerning edit of modern lighting, furniture, and décor. Sourcing more than 400 of the most exceptional international design brands, our curated product assortment features iconic designs as well as the latest in contemporary interior decor. Our team includes certified interior designers and ALA-certified product specialists, and we’re looking for fellow design enthusiasts to join us. Since June 2021, Lumens has been part of Flos B&B Italia Group, a leading global high-end design group operating a portfolio of legacy brands in the world of luxury furnishings and lighting. Driven by a purpose of “We design for a beautiful life,” the Group includes some of the most distinctive European cultural heritage products and designs for the planet, people, and culture. The Group includes FLOS, B&B Italia, Louis Poulsen, Maxalto, Arclinea, Azucena, FENDI Casa, Audo Copenhagen, and Lumens. Commitments: People are at the heart of our business. We strive for an empowering and positive culture and working environment for our people. Therefore, creating a strong, diverse and inclusive team is essential for Lumens. For us, your expertise and motivation matter most. Lumens is also committed to reducing our environmental impact and preserving our planet. We support brands and products with sustainability attributes at their heart, and we are harnessing our scale and ethos of innovation to bring customers services and solutions that respect the environment. For our employees, our initiatives include green campuses and flexible working to reduce our carbon footprint.

title: Junior Financial Analyst (Internship)

title: Asset Management Associate
description: POSITION SUMMARY The Asset Management Associate functions as part of both the asset management and finance team, working with a variety of different individuals who oversee Bear Real Estate Group's owned assets. This role assists asset managers and senior team members with data analysis, reporting, compliance, and day-to-day asset operations to ensure assets are accurately recorded, maintained, and performing as expected. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Financial Reporting & Analysis Support lender and investor reporting on a weekly, monthly, and quarterly basis, working closely with Accounting and Compliance to ensure reports are accurate and submitted on time Prepare financial analyses, documentation, presentations, and summaries for management review Assist with data collection, reporting, and process improvements using tools such as Excel, RealPage Asset Investment Management, Power BI, Smartsheet, and OneDrive Monitor incoming requests for information, responding to or directing inquiries from investors, lenders, and third-party management teams Portfolio Management & Compliance Identify reporting requirements for new acquisitions and financings; update internal trackers and communicate expectations to appropriate team members Support property sale and refinance transactions, coordinating with lenders, legal counsel, accounting, and other stakeholders through the closing process Assist with transitions to third-party property management, including coordination of due diligence items Update the real estate owned schedule to ensure proper insurance coverages are in place Insurance & Risk Management Maintain and monitor the insurance claims tracker to ensure proper documentation, follow-up, and resolution Assist with insurance-related responsibilities including renewals, coverage schedules, certificates, and lender compliance requirements CORE COMPETENCIES Analytical Thinking & Financial Acumen Attention to Detail & Accuracy Strong Communication & Stakeholder Management Organization & Multi-Tasking Collaboration & Independent Work Technical Proficiency & Systems Aptitude QUALIFICATIONS Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Real Estate, Business, or related field preferred 0–3 years of experience in asset management, finance, operations, or a related role Advanced skills in Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, formulas, data analysis) Experience with RealPage, Power BI, and Smartsheet preferred Excellent interpersonal, written, and oral communication skills Attention to detail with the ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities Ability to work collaboratively and independently on multiple projects Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills Knowledge of real estate operations, property management, and compliance preferred

title: Data Scientist
description: About the Role We're looking for an innovative, passionate, and self-motivated individual with an obsession for data, products and uncovering insights, with extensive startup and/or eCommerce experience. You will be partnering with our teams from product, marketing, design, and engineering, to support the growth of our products and business. As our first dedicated data hire, you will be the key player in our data success - organizing our data, turning data into insights, and turning insights into action to solve our business problems. Data @ Amaze As our Data Scientist at Amaze, you will blaze the trail for how we organize and utilize our existing and future data. Collecting raw data from various sources and preparing them in such a way that they can be interpreted by technical and non-technical business people. Applying the generated insights to concrete business problems and solving these business problems together with business stakeholders. Utilizing various methods including data visualization, story-telling, advanced analysis, or simple data feeds to uncover opportunities for growth and optimization. We don’t want you to just be a resource that answers questions - we want you to act as a genuine data partner with your team and the broader business. We have a lot of varied data and need people who can use their skills to help us make the most of it! We also need your extensive startup, collaboration and leadership experience to build the foundations for our future data teams. This is a huge opportunity to grow alongside our fast-growing company and be remembered as one of the reasons Amaze succeeded in its mission to Help anyone sell anything, anywhere.​ This role will act as the driver of our data strategy. What you’ll do: -Become an expert on our business and develop the analytics, data models, dashboards, and reporting relating to it with a heavy emphasis on providing necessary data to the marketing team -Review, optimize, and propose, tools capturing data across the full customer journey; manage all BI tools -Work with stakeholders to ensure that they understand their part of the product or business and how it is performing -Find insights that suggest future directions and improvements in the product and business. Work with  Product, Marketing, Engineering, and our third-party tooling providers to ensure that our data is up-to-date and error-free -Hands-on development, tuning, and overall management of our Data platforms Establishing data principles, and practices to plan, manage, and deliver solutions - ensuring integrity, quality and timeliness of data. -Training, mentoring and supporting teams to gain a data mindset, and understand data principles, and practices. Extend data literacy and curiosity throughout the whole company through education projects -Responsibility for the data considerations during the development of new features, functionality, and optimization from raw data ingestion, to the access and modeling layer, through to maintaining the visualization layer -Providing functional and technical subject matter expertise with respect to data management, business intelligence, and reporting. Establish close partnership with leadership—especially Product, Engineering, and Marketing—to define the strategic data architecture and roadmap aligned to business strategy. Apply product-embedded data science practices, including event- and behavioral-data modeling and support for AI- and ML-driven product features through signal design, evaluation, and feedback loops, while managing planning and delivery of data projects and initiatives across the wider business. -Evaluate existing and new Data solutions to ensure they support current business needs and growth. -Work closely with stakeholders to align technologies and capabilities with business strategies while delivering pragmatic excellence when supporting business data needs by leveraging the existing platforms and architecture patterns where possible. -Have an end-to-end vision, and see how a logical design will translate into one or more physical data models, and how the data will flow through the layered architecture within the data landscape. -Evaluate and renew implemented data solutions, to ensure their relevance and effectiveness in supporting business needs and growth. -Emphasize the importance of and ensure that data management practices are embedded in all data solutions. -Ultimately, establish and grow the data area of business at Amaze to support our big vision. What we’re looking for: -7+ Years of Data/Engineering Experience including experience building and leading teams. -Extensive and proven experience in startups - bring clarity to projects, teams and data to achieve business goals and fast-paced growth. Experience working with marketing teams to help accelerate company growth and double down on marketing programs that are contributing to revenue growth. -Strong competency with SQL, and Tableau and like tools; experience with data-warehouses such as Snowflake, FiveTran, or BigQuery is a plus! -Experience wrangling and analyzing data including migrating from client to server-side data solutions. -Experience with optimizing product funnels, running experiments, and building dashboards that effectively communicate results and outcomes. -Experience working with a broad variety of teams from marketing to engineering. -Familiarity with a wide set of Data Warehouse architectures and methodologies, schemas, and performance/optimization principles -Strong technical knowledge of data integration, metadata and BI analytics tools. -Strong communication, influencing and interpersonal skills. An engaging personality who can take the business on the journey with them. Be able to break down complex topics into simple solutions and ideas -Highly organized, self-motivated, proactive and able to plan. -Ability to analyze, understand complex problems and explain technical information in business terms. -Experience using Snowflake, Google Analytics,,, Tableau, PowerBI or similar BI/data visualization tools Strong background in AI, specifically with dbt Semantic Layer and similar technologies -Comfortable working with stakeholders to build up accurate reports on their areas of the business -Experience with establishing A/B testing and experiment analysis is a plus -Experience with very large data sets is a plus. Any prior experience with creative data visualization or design is also valuable. -It will require close coordination with several stakeholders and a real sense of drive and initiative to keep things moving. Nobody tells you what to do; you identify the opportunities where your team can make the largest impact and you see it through.
